sn47.10:1.1: Then Venerable Ānanda, having dressed in the morning and taken his bowl and robe, went to a certain *bhikkhunis*' lodging; having gone there, he sat down on the prepared seat.
sn47.10:1.2: Then several *bhikkhunis* went to Venerable Ānanda; having gone there, they paid homage to Venerable Ānanda and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, those *bhikkhunis* said this to Venerable Ānanda:
sn47.10:10.2: Satisfied, Venerable Ānanda rejoiced in the *Bhagavā*'s statement.
sn47.10:10.4: The Ambapālī Chapter is the first.
sn47.10:11.2: Sālā, and the heap of the wholesome;
sn47.10:11.3: The falcon, the monkey, the cook,
sn47.10:11.4: The sick one, and the *bhikkhunis*' lodging.
sn47.10:2.1: "Here, *bhante* Ānanda, several *bhikkhunis*, dwelling with their minds well established in the four *satipaṭṭhānas*, perceive a lofty distinction, each one beyond the former."
sn47.10:2.2: "So it is, sisters, so it is, sisters.
sn47.10:2.3: For, sisters, any *bhikkhu* or *bhikkhuni* who dwells with mind well established in the four *satipaṭṭhānas*, this is to be expected of them:
sn47.10:2.4: ‘They will perceive a lofty distinction, each one beyond the former.’"
sn47.10:3.1: Then Venerable Ānanda, having instructed, encouraged, inspired, and gladdened those *bhikkhunis* with a talk on *dhamma*, rose from his seat and departed.
sn47.10:3.2: Then Venerable Ānanda, having walked for alms in Sāvatthī, after the meal, on returning from the almsround, went to the *Bhagavā*; having gone there, he paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, Venerable Ānanda said this to the *Bhagavā*:
sn47.10:4.1: "Here, *bhante*, having dressed in the morning and taken my bowl and robe, I went to a certain *bhikkhunis*' lodging; having gone there, I sat down on the prepared seat.
sn47.10:4.2: Then, *bhante*, several *bhikkhunis* came to me; having come, they paid homage to me and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, *bhante*, those *bhikkhunis* said this to me:
sn47.10:4.3: ‘Here, *bhante* Ānanda, several *bhikkhunis*, dwelling with their minds well established in the four *satipaṭṭhānas*, perceive a lofty distinction, each one beyond the former.’
sn47.10:4.4: When this was said, *bhante*, I said this to those *bhikkhunis*:
sn47.10:4.5: ‘So it is, sisters, so it is, sisters.
sn47.10:4.6: For, sisters, any *bhikkhu* or *bhikkhuni* who dwells with mind well established in the four *satipaṭṭhānas*, this is to be expected of them — they will perceive a lofty distinction, each one beyond the former.’"
sn47.10:5.1: "So it is, Ānanda, so it is, Ānanda.
sn47.10:5.2: For, Ānanda, any *bhikkhu* or *bhikkhuni* who dwells with mind well established in the four *satipaṭṭhānas*, this is to be expected of them:
sn47.10:5.3: ‘They will perceive a lofty distinction, each one beyond the former.’
sn47.10:6.11: ‘The purpose for which I directed the mind [*cittaṁ*] has been fulfilled for me.
sn47.10:6.12: Come now, I will withdraw it.’
sn47.10:6.13: He withdraws it, and he does not think [*vitakketi*] or examine [*vicāreti*].
sn47.10:6.14: He understands: ‘I am without thought [*avitakkomhi*], without examination [*avicāro*], inwardly with *sati* [*satimā*], and I am with *sukha*.’
sn47.10:6.2: Here, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu* dwells contemplating body in body, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed covetousness and distress regarding the world.
sn47.10:6.3: As he dwells contemplating body in body, bodily fever arises in him on account of the body, or sluggishness of mind, or the mind [*cittaṁ*] is distracted outwardly.
sn47.10:6.4: Then, Ānanda, that *bhikkhu* should direct the mind [*cittaṁ*] to some inspiring *nimitta*.
sn47.10:6.5: As he directs the mind [*cittaṁ*] to some inspiring *nimitta*, gladness arises.
sn47.10:6.6: For one who is glad, *pīti* arises.
sn47.10:6.7: For one with a mind of *pīti*, the body becomes tranquil.
sn47.10:6.9: For one with *sukha*, the mind [*cittaṁ*] becomes endowed with *samādhi*.
sn47.10:7.1: Furthermore, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu* dwells contemplating *vedanā* in *vedanā* …
sn47.10:7.10: For one with *sukha*, the mind [*cittaṁ*] becomes endowed with *samādhi*.
sn47.10:7.12: ‘The purpose for which I directed the mind [*cittaṁ*] has been fulfilled for me.
sn47.10:7.13: Come now, I will withdraw it.’
sn47.10:7.14: He withdraws it, and he does not think [*vitakketi*] or examine [*vicāreti*].
sn47.10:7.15: He understands: ‘I am without thought [*avitakkomhi*], without examination [*avicāro*], inwardly with *sati* [*satimā*], and I am with *sukha*.’
sn47.10:7.16: This, Ānanda, is development [*bhāvanā*] by directing.
sn47.10:7.3: he dwells contemplating *dhammas* in *dhammas*, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed covetousness and distress regarding the world.
sn47.10:7.4: As he dwells contemplating *dhammas* in *dhammas*, bodily fever arises in him on account of *dhammas*, or sluggishness of mind, or the mind [*cittaṁ*] is distracted outwardly.
sn47.10:7.5: Then, Ānanda, that *bhikkhu* should direct the mind [*cittaṁ*] to some inspiring *nimitta*.
sn47.10:7.6: As he directs the mind [*cittaṁ*] to some inspiring *nimitta*, gladness arises.
sn47.10:7.7: For one who is glad, *pīti* arises.
sn47.10:7.8: For one with a mind of *pīti*, the body becomes tranquil.
sn47.10:8.1: And how, Ānanda, is there development [*bhāvanā*] without directing?
sn47.10:8.10: And further, he understands: ‘I dwell contemplating mind [*citte*] in mind [*citta*], ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], and I am with *sukha*.’
sn47.10:8.11: Without directing the mind [*cittaṁ*] outwardly, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu* understands: ‘My mind [*cittaṁ*] is not directed outwardly.’
sn47.10:8.12: Then afterwards he understands: ‘It is not contracted, liberated, not directed.’
sn47.10:8.13: And further, he understands: ‘I dwell contemplating *dhammas* in *dhammas*, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], and I am with *sukha*.’
sn47.10:8.14: This, Ānanda, is development [*bhāvanā*] without directing.
sn47.10:8.2: Without directing the mind [*cittaṁ*] outwardly, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu* understands: ‘My mind [*cittaṁ*] is not directed outwardly.’
sn47.10:8.3: Then afterwards he understands: ‘It is not contracted, liberated, not directed.’
sn47.10:8.4: And further, he understands: ‘I dwell contemplating body in body, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], and I am with *sukha*.’
sn47.10:8.5: Without directing the mind [*cittaṁ*] outwardly, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu* understands: ‘My mind [*cittaṁ*] is not directed outwardly.’
sn47.10:8.6: Then afterwards he understands: ‘It is not contracted, liberated, not directed.’
sn47.10:8.7: And further, he understands: ‘I dwell contemplating *vedanā* in *vedanā*, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], and I am with *sukha*.’
sn47.10:8.8: Without directing the mind [*cittaṁ*] outwardly, Ānanda, a *bhikkhu* understands: ‘My mind [*cittaṁ*] is not directed outwardly.’
sn47.10:8.9: Then afterwards he understands: ‘It is not contracted, liberated, not directed.’
sn47.10:9.1: Thus, Ānanda, development [*bhāvanā*] by directing has been taught by me, and development [*bhāvanā*] without directing has been taught.
sn47.10:9.2: What should be done by a teacher seeking the welfare of his disciples, compassionate out of compassion, that I have done for you.
sn47.10:9.3: Here, Ānanda, are the roots of trees; here are empty huts. Meditate [*jhāyatha*], Ānanda; do not be negligent; do not later become remorseful. This is our instruction to you."
